name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayah

```arabic
ЩӮЩҸЩ„Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§Ы  ШЁЩҺШҙЩҺШұЩҢ Щ…Щ‘ЩҗШ«Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШӯЩҺЩүЩ°Щ“ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩүЩ‘ЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩҢ ЩҲЩҺЩ°ШӯЩҗШҜЩҢ Ы– ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶ ЩғЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШұЩ’Ш¬ЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ Щ„ЩҗЩӮЩҺШ§Щ“ШЎЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗЫҰ ЩҒЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩ„ЩӢШ§ ШөЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ„ЩҗШӯЩӢШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҸШҙЩ’ШұЩҗЩғЩ’ ШЁЩҗШ№ЩҗШЁЩҺШ§ШҜЩҺШ©Щҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗЫҰЩ“ ШЈЩҺШӯЩҺШҜЩӢЫўШ§ (ЩЎЩЎЩ )
```

```english
Say, вҖҳI am just a human being like you. It has been revealed to me that your God is the One God. So whoever expects to encounter his LordвҖ”let him act righteously, and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord.вҖҷ (110)
```

## (Commentary)

This verse is the conclusion of the Surah and summarizes the objective of the discourse within it. It has gathered the three foundations of religion (ШЈШөЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҠЩҶ): monotheism (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҲШӯЩҠШҜ) вҖ” *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts*, prophethood (Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҲШ©), and the return (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№Ш§ШҜ) вҖ” *the resurrection and final judgment*. Monotheism is contained in His saying: **ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮЩҢ ЩҲШ§ШӯЩҗШҜЩҢ** "that your God is the one God"; prophethood is in His saying: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ ШЁЩҺШҙЩҺШұЩҢ Щ…ЩҗШ«Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШӯЩү ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ‘ЩҺ** "I am only a mortal like you; it has been revealed to me," and His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ШөШ§Щ„ЩҗШӯШ§ЩӢ** "let him act righteously" etc.; and the return is in His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШұЩ’Ш¬ЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҗЩӮШ§ШЎЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ** "So whoever expects to encounter his Lord."

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ЩӮЩҸЩ„Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ ШЁЩҺШҙЩҺШұЩҢ Щ…ЩҗШ«Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҠЩҸЩҲШӯЩү ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ‘ЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮЩҢ ЩҲШ§ШӯЩҗШҜЩҢ** "Say, вҖҳI am only a mortal like you; it has been revealed to me that your God is the one God.вҖҷ" The first restriction (Ш§Щ„ЩӮШөШұ) limits him ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ to a humanity identical to the humanity of other people; he does not exceed them in anything, nor does he claim such for himself. This was in response to what they used to imagineвҖ”that if he claimed prophethood, he was claiming a divine nature (ЩғЩҠЩҶЩҲЩҶШ© ШҘЩ„ЩҮЩҠШ©) or a supernatural power (ЩӮШҜШұШ© ШәЩҠШЁЩҠШ©). For this reason, they used to propose demands to him regarding things that none knows but Allah and none is capable of but Allah. However, he ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ denied all of that from himself by the command of Allah, and established nothing for himself except that it is revealed to him.

The second restriction limits the God who is their God to being one God, which is monotheism (Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҲШӯЩҠШҜ) declaring that the God of all is one God.

And His saying: **ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШұЩ’Ш¬ЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҗЩӮШ§ШЎЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩҒЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„Щ’** "So whoever expects to encounter his Lord, let him act..." etc., contains a summary of the religious call (Ш§Щ„ШҜШ№ЩҲШ© Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҠЩҶЩҠШ©).

This consists of righteous deeds (Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ…Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШөШ§Щ„Шӯ) performed for the sake of Allah alone, without any partner. He has made this contingent upon the hope of encountering the Lord, the Exalted, which is the return (Ш§Щ„ШұШ¬ЩҲШ№) to Him. For if it were not for the reckoning (Ш§Щ„ШӯШіШ§ШЁ) and the recompense (Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШІШ§ШЎ), there would be no motive calling one to adopt the religion or to commit to belief and action, as the Exalted said: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШ¶ЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ШіЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ„Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺШ°Ш§ШЁЩҢ ШҙЩҺШҜЩҗЩҠШҜЩҢ ШЁЩҗЩ…Ш§ ЩҶЩҺШіЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ…ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҗШіШ§ШЁЩҗ** "Indeed those who stray from the way of AllahвҖ”there is a severe punishment for them because they forgot the Day of Reckoning" (38:26).

He has arranged righteous deeds and the absence of polytheism (Ш§Щ„ШҘШҙШұШ§Щғ) in the worship of the Lord upon the belief in the return. This is because belief in oneness (Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯШҜШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©) alongside polytheism in action are two contradictory things that cannot coexist; if the God, the Exalted, is one, then He is one in all His attributes, including being the sole object of worship (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№ШЁЩҲШҜЩҠШ©), having no partner therein.

He has based the adoption of religion upon the "hope" (ШұШ¬Ш§ШЎ) of the return rather than "certainty" (Ш§Щ„ЩӮШ·Ш№) of it, because the mere possibility (Ш§ШӯШӘЩ…Ш§Щ„) of it is sufficient for the obligation of taking precaution against it, based on the principle of the necessity of averting potential harm (ШҜЩҒШ№ Ш§Щ„Ш¶ШұШұ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӯШӘЩ…Щ„). It has also been said that "encounter" (Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩӮШ§ШЎ) refers to the encounter of honor (Щ„ЩӮШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„ЩғШұШ§Щ…Ш©), which is hoped for but not definitively guaranteed.

He has made the hope of encountering Allah contingent upon His saying: **ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮЩҸЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҮЩҢ ЩҲШ§ШӯЩҗШҜЩҢ** "that your God is the one God," because the return of the servants to Allah, the Glorified, is part of the complete meaning of divinity (Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ„ЩҲЩҮЩҠШ©). To Him, the Exalted, belongs every sought perfection and every beautiful attribute, among which are acting with truth (ЩҒШ№Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩӮ) and judging with justice (Ш§Щ„ШӯЩғЩ… ШЁШ§Щ„Ш№ШҜЩ„). Both of these necessitate the return of His servants to Him and the passing of judgment between them. The Exalted said: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ Ш®ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩ’ЩҶЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ‘ЩҺЩ…Ш§ШЎЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШұЩ’Ш¶ЩҺ ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Ш§ ШЁШ§Ш·ЩҗЩ„Ш§ЩӢ Ш°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШёЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩғЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҺЩҲЩҺЩҠЩ’Щ„ЩҢ Щ„ЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩғЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ШұЩҗ ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ ЩҶЩҺШ¬Щ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҲЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§Щ„ЩҗШӯШ§ШӘЩҗ ЩғЩҺШ§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҒЩ’ШіЩҗШҜЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШұЩ’Ш¶Щҗ ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ ЩҶЩҺШ¬Щ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩӮЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ЩғЩҺШ§Щ„Щ’ЩҒЩҸШ¬Щ‘ЩҺШ§ШұЩҗ** "We did not create the heaven and the earth and whatever is between them in vain. That is the opinion of those who are faithless. So woe to the faithless from the Fire! Shall We treat those who have faith and do righteous deeds like those who cause corruption on the earth? Shall We treat the God-conscious like the vicious?" (38:27-28).

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Mandah, Abu NuвҖҷaym in *al-Sahaba*, and Ibn вҖҳAsakir narrated via al-Suddi al-Saghir, from al-Kalbi, from Abu Salih, from Ibn вҖҳAbbas, who said: "Jundub ibn Zuhayr, whenever he prayed, fasted, or gave charity and was mentioned favorably for it, he would feel pleased by it and increase in those acts due to the talk of the people. So Allah censured him, and regarding this, there was revealed: **ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШұЩ’Ш¬ЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҗЩӮШ§ШЎЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩҒЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ШөШ§Щ„ЩҗШӯШ§ЩӢ ЩҲЩҺЩ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШҙЩ’ШұЩҗЩғЩ’ ШЁЩҗШ№ЩҗШЁШ§ШҜЩҺШ©Щҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ ШЈЩҺШӯЩҺШҜШ§ЩӢ** 'So whoever expects to encounter his Lord, let him act righteously, and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord.'"

**I say:** Something similar has been mentioned in several other narrations without mentioning the name. It should be understood as the application (Ш§ЩҶШ·ШЁШ§ЩӮ) of the verse to the case, for it is unlikely that the conclusion of one of the Surahs would be revealed solely for a specific personal reason.

In the same source, from Abu Hatim, from SaвҖҷid ibn Jubayr regarding the verse, he said: "The Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ said: 'Indeed your Lord says: I am the best of partners (Ш®ЩҠШұ ШҙШұЩҠЩғ). Whoever associates anyone from My creation with Me in his deed, I leave the entire deed to that partner, and I do not accept except what was purely for Me.' Then the Prophet ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ recited: **ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШұЩ’Ш¬ЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҗЩӮШ§ШЎЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩҒЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ШөШ§Щ„ЩҗШӯШ§ЩӢ ЩҲЩҺЩ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШҙЩ’ШұЩҗЩғЩ’ ШЁЩҗШ№ЩҗШЁШ§ШҜЩҺШ©Щҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ ШЈЩҺШӯЩҺШҜШ§ЩӢ** 'So whoever expects to encounter his Lord, let him act righteously, and not associate anyone with the worship of his Lord.'"

In *Tafsir al-Ayyashi*, from вҖҳAli ibn Salim, from Abu вҖҳAbdillah Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, who said: "Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, said: 'I am the best of partners. Whoever associates [another] with Me in his deed, I do not accept it except what was purely for Me.'"

Al-Ayyashi said: In another narration from him Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢШ§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, he said: "Indeed Allah says: 'I am the best of partners. Whoever acts for Me and for another, then it is for the one he acted for, and not for Me.'"

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ahmad, Ibn Abi al-Dunya, Ibn Mardawayh, al-Hakim (who authenticated it), and al-Bayhaqi narrated from Shaddad ibn Aws, who said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ saying: 'Whoever prays to show off (ЩҠШұШ§ШҰЩҠ) has committed polytheism (ШЈШҙШұЩғ); whoever fasts to show off has committed polytheism; and whoever gives charity to show off has committed polytheism.' Then he recited: **ЩҒЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШұЩ’Ш¬ЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҗЩӮШ§ШЎЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩҮЩҗ** 'So whoever expects to encounter his Lord...' the verse."

In *Tafsir al-Ayyashi*, from Zurarah and Humran, from Abu JaвҖҷfar and Abu вҖҳAbdillah Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…, they both said: "If a servant performs a deed seeking thereby the mercy of Allah and the Abode of the Hereafter, but then introduces into it the pleasure of any person, he is a polytheist (Щ…ШҙШұЩғ)."

**I say:** The narrations in this chapter from the paths of both the ShiвҖҷa and the Sunnis are beyond counting. What is meant by polytheism (Ш§Щ„ШҙШұЩғ) in them is the hidden polytheism (Ш§Щ„ШҙШұЩғ Ш§Щ„Ш®ЩҒЩҠ) which does not contradict the essence of faith (ШЈШөЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҠЩ…Ш§ЩҶ), but rather its perfection. The Exalted said: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҸ ШЈЩҺЩғЩ’Ш«ЩҺШұЩҸЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШЁЩҗШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҸШҙЩ’ШұЩҗЩғЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** "And most of them do not believe in Allah without ascribing partners to Him" (12:106). Thus, the verse includes this by its inner meaning (ШЁШ§Ш·ЩҶЩҮШ§), not by its literal occasion of revelation (ШӘЩҶШІЩҠЩ„ЩҮШ§).

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, al-Tabarani and Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Abu Hakim, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ said: 'If nothing had been revealed to my nation except the conclusion of Surah al-Kahf, it would have sufficed them.'"

**I say:** The reason for this was previously mentioned in the preceding commentary.

Completed, and praise be to Allah.
